title: Wiggly Interconnectedness
definition: The idea that everything in the universe is inherently interconnected, even at a fundamental level.

difficulty_levels.beginner: It's like every piece of the world is connected by an invisible web, and each piece affects all the others in some way.
difficulty_levels.intermediate: This concept posits that everything in the universe, from the smallest subatomic particles to the largest galaxies, are interconnected and influence each other through complex networks and relationships.
difficulty_levels.expert: Wiggly Interconnectedness suggests that the fundamental laws of physics allow for a deep interdependence between seemingly disparate aspects of reality. This concept challenges traditional reductionist views of science and encourages a more holistic understanding of the universe.

extra_fields.insight: The profound realization that our world is inherently connected at its core, and that even our thoughts and actions ripple outwards in ways we may never fully comprehend.
